title: db migration version read vs apply race: version snapshot used across all migrations
description: db.rs WalletStore::new: reads PRAGMA user_version once into 'version' variable, then uses that snapshot in 8 consecutive 'if version < N' guards. Each migration updates user_version at commit. If a second process opens the same DB file concurrently during the first process's migration sequence, the second process may read a version between 1 and 7 and re-enter a partially-executed migration (e.g., version=3 snapshot, then try to run migrations 4-8 which the first process already ran). SQLite serializes writers so the ALTER TABLE/CREATE TABLE calls will be safe (IF NOT EXISTS), but the PRAGMA user_version update in the first process's transaction is only visible after commit. If the pool max_connections=1 enforcement is the only guard, a second WalletStore::new from a different process can race. Note: warn on version mismatch guard (nie-qdlu.11) is already open.
